title: Bind tk board to 127.0.0.1 by default with --host override
description:
Context: tk board currently binds the wildcard address (carried over from tk run --board); the final epic review flagged localhost binding as hardening since the board is unauthenticated. Change bindBoardListener in cmd/tk/cmd/board.go to bind 127.0.0.1:<port> by default and add a --host flag (e.g. --host 0.0.0.0 to expose on LAN; --host also feeds the printed URL). Update the port-collision fail-fast test expectations if the bind address changes their setup; add a test that the default listener is loopback-only and that --host 0.0.0.0 binds wildcard. Update docs: README Web Board section and skills/ticks/references/tk-commands.md flag table (add --host row, note the localhost default). Remember ResetFlags in cmd/tk/cmd/root.go for the new flag var.

Run: go test ./cmd/tk/...
acceptance_criteria: Default bind is 127.0.0.1 (test-verified); --host overrides and is documented in README + tk-commands.md; collision fail-fast behavior preserved; go test ./... green
